subject: Office printer shows offline on Mac only
description: Printer works for colleagues on Windows; this Mac shows printer offline in Print dialog.

ts_steps.title: Open print queue
ts_steps.action: System Settings → Printers & Scanners → printer → Open Print Queue.

ts_steps.title: Clear paused and jobs
ts_steps.action: Uncheck Pause Printer; delete all queued jobs.

ts_steps.title: Print to PDF
ts_steps.action: Any app File → Print → PDF → Save as PDF — confirms local print pipeline.

ts_steps.title: Ping printer IP
ts_steps.action: If IP printer: Terminal ping -c 4 printer_ip — no reply means network not driver.

ts_steps.title: Remove printer
ts_steps.action: Printers & Scanners → Remove Printer / Delete Printer.

ts_steps.title: Add by IP
ts_steps.action: Add → IP → address; Protocol IPP or LPD per admin; queue name if needed.

ts_steps.title: Driver choice
ts_steps.action: Secure AirPrint or vendor PPD; avoid duplicate queue names post-migration.

ts_steps.title: Auth preset
ts_steps.action: Print dialog — select preset with user code or accounting if required.

ts_steps.title: UNC to LPD mapping
ts_steps.action: Ask print admin for lpd:// or ipp:// URL equivalent to Windows shared queue.

ts_steps.title: Reset printing system
ts_steps.action: Control-click list → Reset printing system — removes all printers; re-add only needed.

ts_steps.title: CUPS log
ts_steps.action: Escalate with /var/log/cups/error_log snippet if still offline.

ts_steps.title: Universal driver
ts_steps.action: Install latest vendor driver package labeled Apple Silicon or Universal.

internal_information: IP wrong, paused queue, IPP vs LPD, auth. CUPS same on Apple Silicon with Universal drivers.
